classes.name: premier_league.players.season_leaders.PlayerSeasonLeaders

methods.name: premier_league.players.season_leaders.PlayerSeasonLeaders.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the PlayerSeasonLeaders object.

Args:
    stat_type (Literal['G', 'A']): The type of statistic to scrape ('G' for goals, 'A' for assists).
    target_season (str, optional): The specific season to scrape data for. Defaults to None.

methods.name: premier_league.players.season_leaders.PlayerSeasonLeaders._get_url
documentation:
Generate the URL for scraping based on the stat_type.

Returns:
    str: The URL to scrape data from.

methods.name: premier_league.players.season_leaders.PlayerSeasonLeaders._init_top_stats_table
documentation:
Initialize and process the top statistics table.

This method scrapes the raw data, cleans it, and structures it into a list of lists.

Returns:
    list[list[str]]: Processed list of top players and their statistics.

methods.name: premier_league.players.season_leaders.PlayerSeasonLeaders.get_top_stats_list
documentation:
Get the processed list of top players and their statistics.

Args:
    limit (int, optional): The number of top players to include. Defaults to None.
Returns:
    list: The season_top_players_list attribute.

methods.name: premier_league.players.season_leaders.PlayerSeasonLeaders.get_top_stats_csv
documentation:
Export the top statistics to a CSV file.

Args:
    file_name (str): The name of the file to save (without extension).
    header    (str, optional): The header for the CSV file. Defaults to None.
    limit     (int, optional): The number of top players to include. Defaults to None.

methods.name: premier_league.players.season_leaders.PlayerSeasonLeaders.get_top_stats_json
documentation:
Export the top statistics to a JSON file.

Args:
    file_name (str): The name of the file to save (without extension).
    header    (str, optional): The header for the JSON file. Defaults to None.
    limit     (int, optional): The number of top players to include. Defaults to None.

methods.name: premier_league.players.season_leaders.PlayerSeasonLeaders.get_top_stats_pdf
documentation:
Export the top 20 player statistics to a PDF file.

This method creates a formatted PDF with a title, table of statistics,
and applies styling to enhance readability.

Args:
    file_name (str): The name of the file to save (without extension).
    path (str): The path to save the PDF file

A class to scrape and process top player statistics (goals or assists) for a Premier League season.

This class inherits from BaseScrapper and provides methods to retrieve, process, and export
player statistics in various formats (list, CSV, JSON, PDF).

Attributes:
    stat_type (Literal['G', 'A']): The type of statistic to scrape ('G' for goals, 'A' for assists).
    stat_url (str): The URL to scrape data from.
    page: The scraped web page content.
    season_top_players_list (list): Processed list of top players and their statistics.

classes.name: premier_league.base.BaseScrapper

methods.name: premier_league.base.BaseScrapper.__post_init__
documentation:
Initialize the current and previous seasons based on the current date or target season.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the target_season is invalid or in an incorrect format.

methods.name: premier_league.base.BaseScrapper.make_request
documentation:
Make an HTTP GET request to the specified URL.

Returns:
    Response: The HTTP response object.

Raises:
    HTTPException: If an error occurs during the request.

methods.name: premier_league.base.BaseScrapper.parse_to_html
documentation:
Parse the HTTP response content into a BeautifulSoup object.

Returns:
    BeautifulSoup: The parsed HTML content.

methods.name: premier_league.base.BaseScrapper.convert_to_xml
documentation:
Convert a BeautifulSoup object to an lxml ElementTree.

Args:
    bsoup (BeautifulSoup): The BeautifulSoup object to convert.

Returns:
    ElementTree: The converted XML tree.

methods.name: premier_league.base.BaseScrapper.additional_scrapper
documentation:
Create a new BaseScrapper instance for an additional URL without creating a new object.

Args:
    additional_url (str): The URL to scrape.

Returns:
    BaseScrapper: A new BaseScrapper instance with the page loaded.

methods.name: premier_league.base.BaseScrapper.request_url_page
documentation:
Request the URL and parse it into an XML ElementTree.

Returns:
    ElementTree: The parsed XML representation of the web page.

methods.name: premier_league.base.BaseScrapper.get_list_by_xpath
documentation:
Get a list of elements matching the given XPath.

Args:
    xpath (str): The XPath query to execute.
    clean (bool, optional): Whether to clean the text content of the elements. Defaults to True.

Returns:
    Optional[list]: A list of matching elements, or an empty list if no matches are found.

methods.name: premier_league.base.BaseScrapper.get_text_by_xpath
documentation:
Get text content from elements matching the given XPath.

This method provides various ways to select and manipulate the matched elements.

Args:
    xpath (str): The XPath query to execute.
    pos (int, optional): The position of the element to return. Defaults to 0.
    index (int, optional): The index of the element to return.
    index_from (int, optional): The starting index for slicing the result list.
    index_to (int, optional): The ending index for slicing the result list.
    join_str (str, optional): A string to join multiple elements if returned.

Returns:
    Optional[str]: The extracted text content, or None if no match is found.

A base class for web scraping operations.

This class provides methods for making HTTP requests, parsing HTML content,
and extracting data using XPath queries.

Attributes:
    url (str): The URL to scrape.
    page (ElementTree): The parsed XML representation of the web page.

classes.name: premier_league.ranking.ranking_table.RankingTable

methods.name: premier_league.ranking.ranking_table.RankingTable.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the RankingTable instance.

Args:
    target_season (str, optional): The specific season to scrape data for.
                                   If not provided, the current season is used.

methods.name: premier_league.ranking.ranking_table.RankingTable._init_ranking_table
documentation:
Initialize the ranking table by scraping and processing the data.

Returns:
    list: A list of lists containing the processed ranking data.

methods.name: premier_league.ranking.ranking_table.RankingTable.get_prem_ranking_list
documentation:
Get the Premier League ranking list.

Returns:
    list: The processed ranking data.

methods.name: premier_league.ranking.ranking_table.RankingTable.get_prem_ranking_csv
documentation:
Save the Premier League ranking data to a CSV file.

Args:
    file_name (str): The name of the file to save the data to (without extension).
    header    (str): The header to include in the CSV file.

methods.name: premier_league.ranking.ranking_table.RankingTable.get_prem_ranking_json
documentation:
Save the Premier League ranking data to a JSON file.

Args:
    file_name (str): The name of the file to save the data to (without extension).
    header    (str): The header to include in the JSON file.

methods.name: premier_league.ranking.ranking_table.RankingTable.get_prem_ranking_dict
documentation:
Get the Premier League ranking data as a dictionary.

Args:
    header (str): The header to include in the dictionary. (Parent Key for the entire data)

Returns:
    dict: The processed ranking data as a dictionary.

methods.name: premier_league.ranking.ranking_table.RankingTable.get_prem_ranking_pdf
documentation:
Generate a PDF file containing the Premier League ranking table.

This method creates a formatted PDF file with the ranking table, including
color-coded rows for European qualification spots and relegation.

Args:
    file_name (str): The name of the file to save the PDF to (without extension).
    dir (str): The directory to save the PDF file to.

methods.name: premier_league.ranking.ranking_table.RankingTable._find_european_qualification_spot

params.name: tuple[str, tuple[int, int], tuple[int, int]] | list

documentation:
Determine the European qualification spots for the current season.

This method analyzes the current ranking and determines which teams qualify for
various European competitions based on their league position and cup performances.

Returns:
    list: A list of tuples containing styling information for the PDF table.

methods.name: premier_league.ranking.ranking_table.RankingTable._find_european_competition_spot
documentation:
Find the winners of various competitions that may affect European qualification.

This method scrapes data for FA Cup, EFL Cup, Champions League, Europa League,
and Europa Conference League winners for the current season.

Returns:
    dict: A dictionary containing the winners of each competition.

methods.name: premier_league.ranking.ranking_table.RankingTable._scrap_european_qualification_spot
documentation:
Scrape European qualification spots for seasons prior to 2019-20.

This method is used for older seasons where the qualification rules were different.

Returns:
    list: A list of tuples containing styling information for the PDF table.

methods.name: premier_league.ranking.ranking_table.RankingTable._is_team_in_european_competition
documentation:
Check if a team is already qualified for a European competition.

Args:
    team_index (int): The index of the team to check.
    competition_indices (list): Indices of teams already qualified for a competition.
    all_teams (list): List of all teams in the current premier league.

Returns:
    list: A list of indices where the team appears in the competition qualifiers.

methods.name: premier_league.ranking.ranking_table.RankingTable._find_tournament_winner
documentation:
Find the winner of a specific tournament from a scraped page.

Args:
    cup_page: The scraped page containing the tournament information.
    xpath (str): The XPath to locate the winner information.

Returns:
    str: The name of the tournament winner, or None if not found.

A class to scrape and process Premier League ranking data.

This class inherits from BaseScrapper and provides methods to retrieve,
process, and output Premier League ranking data in various formats.

Attributes:
    season (str): The current Premier League season.
    target_season (str): The specific season to scrape data for, if provided.
    page: The scraped web page containing the ranking data.
    ranking_list (list): The processed ranking data.

classes.name: premier_league.transfers.transfers.TeamNotFoundError
documentation:
Exception raised when a team is not found in the specified Premier League season.

Attributes:
    team (str): The name of the team that was not found.
    season (str): The season in which the team was searched for.

classes.name: premier_league.transfers.transfers.Transfers

methods.name: premier_league.transfers.transfers.Transfers.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Transfers object.

Args:
    target_season (str, optional): The target season for transfer data. Defaults to None.

methods.name: premier_league.transfers.transfers.Transfers._init_transfers_table
documentation:
Initialize the transfers table by scraping and processing the data.

Returns:
    dict: A dictionary containing processed transfer data for all teams.

methods.name: premier_league.transfers.transfers.Transfers.print_transfer_table
documentation:
Print the transfer table for a specified team.

Args:
    team (str): The name of the team.

Raises:
    TeamNotFoundError: If the specified team is not found in the current season.

methods.name: premier_league.transfers.transfers.Transfers.transfer_in_table
documentation:
Get the transfer-in table for a specified team.

Args:
    team (str): The name of the team.

Returns:
    list[list[str]]: A list of lists containing transfer-in data.

Raises:
    TeamNotFoundError: If the specified team is not found in the current season.

methods.name: premier_league.transfers.transfers.Transfers.transfer_out_table
documentation:
Get the transfer-out table for a specified team.

Args:
    team (str): The name of the team.

Returns:
    list[list[str]]: A list of lists containing transfer-out data.

Raises:
    TeamNotFoundError: If the specified team is not found in the current season.

methods.name: premier_league.transfers.transfers.Transfers.transfer_csv
documentation:
Export transfer data to a CSV file.

Args:
    team (str): The name of the team.
    file_name (str): The name of the file to export to.
    transfer_type (Literal["in", "out", "both"], optional): The type of transfers to export. Defaults to "both".

Raises:
    TeamNotFoundError: If the specified team is not found in the current season.

methods.name: premier_league.transfers.transfers.Transfers.transfer_json
documentation:
Export transfer data to a JSON file.

Args:
    team (str): The name of the team.
    file_name (str): The name of the file to export to.
    transfer_type (Literal["in", "out", "both"], optional): The type of transfers to export. Defaults to "both".

Raises:
    TeamNotFoundError: If the specified team is not found in the current season.

methods.name: premier_league.transfers.transfers.Transfers.get_all_current_teams
documentation:
Get a list of all teams in the given Premier League Season.

Returns:
    list[str]: A list of team names.

A class for scraping and managing Player transfer data for Premier League teams in a given season.

This class inherits from BaseScrapper and provides methods to retrieve,
display, and export transfer data for specified teams and seasons.

Attributes:
    page: The URL page to be Scrapped.
    _season_top_players (dict): A dictionary containing transfer data for all teams.
